_comment: Blob proof — generalised across K ≥ 1 EIP-4844 blobs in a single proof, fused with the recursive verification of the N execution proofs whose ranges tile the combined block range. Per §2.2 of the rollup_spec Readme. The 14-field public-input tuple matches §2.4 verbatim, so an aggregation proof can consume blob proofs as-is and (if hierarchy is added later, see §2.5) a higher-level aggregation proof can consume aggregation proofs the same way.
_comment_blockRange: Combined block range across all K blobs in this proof. Must equal blobs[0].blockRange.startBlockNumber .. blobs[K-1].blockRange.endBlockNumber, with no gaps between consecutive blob ranges (continuity is enforced by the guest via the per-blob shnarf chain plus the execution-proof block-hash sequence — see §2.2 steps 3 and 4).
_comment_blobs: K = 2 blobs in this example. Order is the canonical on-chain shnarf-chain order. Each entry carries its own KZG binding, the blocks-it-decompresses-to (truncated form per blob.py::TruncatedEthereumBlock), and its block range. The guest performs §2.2 steps 1–3 once per blob: KZG check, decompress, parse, and chain the shnarf via Hash(shnarf_{b-1}, lastBlockHash_b, blobHash_b). K = 1 is also valid and degenerates to the single-blob case.

blockData._comment: blocks 1000502..1000509 omitted in the fixture — the guest receives all m_b = 10 entries
blocks 1000512..1000519 omitted in the fixture — the guest receives all m_b = 10 entries

_comment_shnarfTransition: Overall transition. prevShnarf is the inbound shnarf before blob[0]; newShnarf is the outbound shnarf after blob[K-1]. The guest replays Hash(shnarf_{b-1}, lastBlockHash_b, blobHash_b) for each b ∈ [0, K-1] and asserts the final value equals newShnarf (§2.2 step 3). Intermediate shnarfs are not exposed.
_comment_executionProofs: The N execution proofs that tile the combined block range across all K blobs, in canonical (start..end) order. Each entry is a file path to a serialised execution prover-response JSON — same convention as today's prover/backend/aggregation Request.executionProofs []string. The guest decodes each file, reads proof bytes + 15-field PI tuple + per-block witness (L2ToL1MsgHashes, txFroms, filtered FTX addresses) directly from the response. Continuity (newLastBlockHash, L1L2BridgeRollingHash, dynamicChainConfigHash, newFtxRollingHash) is asserted across consecutive proofs by §2.2 step 9 — including across blob boundaries, which fall implicitly inside this list.
_comment_chainConfig: Single value across the proof. Used to verify the dynamicChainConfigHash bound by every inner execution proof. A chain-config change is a finalisation boundary; consequently a blob proof never spans one.
_comment_publicInputs: The 14-field tuple this blob proof will output. Identical shape to §2.4 (and to getZkAggregatedProof.request.json::expectedAggregatedPublicInputs). Provided here so the coordinator can fail-fast on a mismatch with the response before forwarding the proof to the aggregation step.
